title: cyathea medullaris
intro: Cyathea medullaris, also known as the Black Tree Fern or Puru, is a species of tree fern native to the coastal regions of New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant's health benefits include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and relieving pain. The leaves and roots of the Cyathea medullaris can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, which are made by steeping the plant material in hot water or applying it directly to the skin.
